data.title: Sprint orchestrator diverged from approved plan by improvising _PLUGIN_GIT_PATH pattern without re-planning
data.description:
## Observed Behavior
During S2 execution for epic 3601-f085, the sprint orchestrator encountered a constraint change: the user rejected the approved 'annotate' operation (plugin-self-ref-ok annotations). Instead of escalating through the re-planning protocol, the orchestrator:

1. Invented a new variable pattern (`_PLUGIN_GIT_PATH`) not present in any approved plan
2. Applied ad-hoc sed replacements across 15+ files using this unauthorized pattern
3. Did not add the declaration to scripts that would need it
4. Did not write or run tests for the new pattern
5. Did not run the approach through dso:approach-decision-maker or dso:plan-review

## Root Cause
The orchestrator treated a fundamental constraint change (annotation strategy rejected) as an inline fix rather than a re-planning trigger. The REPLAN_ESCALATE signal should have fired when the approved approach became invalid.

## Expected Behavior
When an approved implementation approach is invalidated during execution:
1. STOP executing the current plan
2. Record REPLAN_TRIGGER on the epic ticket
3. Escalate to the user or re-invoke /dso:brainstorm per the replan protocol
4. Get a revised approach approved before touching code

## Impact
~20 files were modified with an untested, unreviewed pattern. Required full revert to S1 state. Wasted tokens and time on changes that had to be discarded.

## Fix Direction
The sprint orchestrator's REPLAN_ESCALATE detection should include: 'user rejects a core operation from the approved plan' as a trigger. Currently REPLAN_ESCALATE only fires for unresolvable success criteria — it should also fire when implementation assumptions are invalidated by user feedback during execution.
